purpose: Cheap audit — initial filter that decides skip / manual_review / starter_candidate / audit_candidate. Reads only Maps scrape payload + one Tinyfish fetch of the homepage. T0 cost (free) per lead.
philosophy: V1 wrongly penalized https + many-reviews leads as 'too established'. V2 reverses this: the BEST V2 lead is a high-GBP-quality business whose website is old/broken. We score gbp_quality and redesign_need separately, then weight redesign_need higher because that's the V2 sales angle.

stage_0_5_enrichment_gate.name: Stage 0.5 — Enrichment-before-skip gate
stage_0_5_enrichment_gate.description: Before Stage 1, if a lead has ANY usable signal but lacks contact channels, force search enrichment first. Only after enrichment do we decide to skip.
triggers_enrichment_if: has business name AND (no phone AND no website AND no social)
has business name AND category matches niche AND missing key contact
skips_immediately_if: no business name AND no phone AND no website
category mismatches the search query (relevance fail)

stage_1_gbp_triage.name: Stage 1 — GBP triage
stage_1_gbp_triage.description: Reads only Maps scrape payload. Outputs gbp_quality 0-100 and an early skip/continue decision. No network calls.

rules.hard_skip_note: Relevance fail = skip immediately. The 20 points reflect that we strongly require relevance, but the binary outcome matters more than the score.

stage_2_site_quick_scan.name: Stage 2 — Website quick scan
stage_2_site_quick_scan.description: Only runs if has_website. Fetches homepage via Tinyfish (T0, free). Outputs redesign_need 0-100 — higher = the website is more in need of redesign = MORE valuable lead for ProfitsLocal.
stage_2_site_quick_scan.skipped_when: websiteStatus = no_website (becomes starter_candidate via different path)

rules.check: url scheme is http not https
rules.rationale: No HTTPS = trust gap, easy fix, instant before/after wow
Sparse text = empty placeholder site or single-page dead site

rules.check: homepage text does not mention business city/suburb
rules.rationale: No local mention = generic template, weak local SEO + trust

rules.check: phone digits NOT in first 1500 chars of markdown
rules.rationale: Hidden contact = critical UX issue; the #1 sales angle for many leads

rules.check: first 1500 chars lack any of: quote, contact, book, call, get, free
rules.rationale: No call-to-action language = unclear next step

rules.check: markdown lacks 2+ of: Services, About, Contact, Reviews, FAQ
no viewport meta tag in raw HTML head
Tinyfish-extracted image refs imply low quality (alt missing OR url contains /200x/ /150/ /thumb/)

scoring.rationale: redesign_need weighted higher because V2 sells redesign. A site with bad GBP but bad website is still a strong target; a site with great GBP but already-modern website is a weak target for V2.

hard_triggers.rationale: No website + reachable = easiest V2 win (build them anything)
HTTP-only with proven traction = obvious redesign win
Strong GBP traction = lead is valuable regardless of cheap signals; FORCE at minimum into audit_candidate. Fixes the V1 bug where high-review HTTPS leads got skipped.

thresholds.label: Strong V2 candidate — proceed to detailed audit
Marginal — likely too thin but worth a glance

starter_candidate_path.description: Leads with no_website but reachable contact go through a different decision: they're not scored for redesign_need (no site to scan). Instead, gbp_quality alone + Stage 0.5 enrichment determines their value.

decision_rules.if: no_website AND (phone OR email) AND gbp_quality >= 30
decision_rules.rationale: Easy V2 win — build them anything, they currently have nothing
Stage 0.5 — try search before giving up
Thin signal — operator decides if worth more research

ui_visibility.admin_scoring_page: /admin/scoring renders this entire JSON as a readable spec
ui_visibility.admin_leads_breakdown: /admin/leads/<slug> shows the per-rule earn/miss breakdown for a specific lead
ui_visibility.queue_card_criteria: /admin/queue stage cards reference the thresholds + hard_triggers
